free_text: Intermediate structure in 12C+16O system through alpha-
induced reactions on 24Mg in the energy range Ealpha =
Natural Mg of 130 ug/cm2
thickness evaporated on carbon foil of 20 ug/cm2
thickness.
Three surface barrier detectors of depletion
layer thickness ranging from 30 to 47 um were set at
lab angle 30, 40 and 50 degree to the beam,
respectively. These 12C detectors were at a distance of
11.3 cm from the target subtending a solid angle of 3
msr.
For the detection of scattered alpha particles
rectangular surface barrier detector of 500 um
thickness and of dimension 27 x 7 mm2 was set at a mean
angle of 156 degree to the beam in the laboratory. This
detector was set at a distance of 16.3 cm from the
target subtending a solid angle of 7 msr. A beam
monitoring detector of 2 mm thickness was also set at a
forward angle of 10 degree to the beam to detect the
elastically scattered alphas as a check on the beam.
For angular distribution measurements for the
reaction 24Mg(4He,12C)16O four thin surface barrier
detectors of mounted 10 degree apart.
No detail information on sources of
uncertainties.
Aman Sharma and VT
E.T.Mirgule (2018-04-09): Both 12C and 16O are in
their ground states.
